  a = (a₁, a₂, … , aₙ),  b = (b₁, b₂, … , bₙ) ∈ ℝⁿ,  c ∈ ℝ.

Compute the dot product of b with the scalar-multiple c a:

(c a) · b = Σ_{i=1}^{n} (c aᵢ) bᵢ  
      = c Σ_{i=1}^{n} aᵢ bᵢ      (since c is a scalar)  
      = c (a · b).

Hence
 c (a · b) = (c a) · b

for every choice of vectors a, b and scalar c.  
This is a direct consequence of the linearity of the dot product in each argument.

z = r^{2} (\cos^{2} \theta + \sin^{2} \theta) - 1
\]

In spherical coordinates \((\rho, \theta, \phi)\), the relationships with Cartesian coordinates \((x, y, z)\) are:
